title: Election voting advice from AI chatbots ‘inaccurate and unreliable’
excerpt: Research during Hungary election shows AI recommended parties not running and gave highly volatile answers to identical prompts
content_html:
AI chatbots provide inaccurate, inconsistent and unreliable guidance to voters asking which party they should back, a study suggests, often recommending the wrong party, not mentioning the right one, or listing parties not even running.

“The results raise serious concerns about the reliability of general-purpose AI systems in electoral contexts,” the study, published by the civil liberties group Liberties and based on research during this year’s Hungarian parliamentary elections, concluded.

“They misclassified profiles, omitted relevant parties and included parties not running in the election,” said the report, shared with the Guardian. Outcomes were also highly volatile, with “materially different” answers given to identical prompts.

The report’s most striking finding from the Hungarian election – convincingly won by Péter Magyar’s opposition Tisza party – was an overwhelming bias in party visibility, particularly as regards Tisza: in 90% of cases when ChatGPT was fed a detailed Tisza-aligned voter profile, it failed to recommend the party.

Magyar’s landslide brought to an end Viktor Orbán’s 16-year grip on power as prime minister and head of his national-conservative Fidesz party.

The researchers noted that unlike voting advice sites or independent media explainers, general-purpose AI (GPAI) systems did not disclose how they generated political guidance, did not yield reproducible results, and were not subject to election-related public oversight.

However, their responses often look confident and authoritative, making users more likely to trust them. With 29.8% of Hungary’s population identified as AI users, the researchers tested the advice given by the two most popular, ChatGPT and Gemini.

Based on party positions outlined on Voksmonitor, a respected Hungarian voting advice app, Liberties created five distinct voter profiles, each aligned with one of the five parties registered on national lists for Hungary’s parliamentary elections.

Each voter profile was then tested 10 times in ChatGPT and 10 times in Gemini, using two different requests: one for direct advice on which party the fictitious voter should back, and another for a percentage match with each of the five parties.

In percentage-matching tests, ChatGPT assigned Tisza a score in just 2% of cases. Instead, users with Tisza-aligned views were routinely recommended a smaller party unlikely to cross the 5% threshold for parliament, or parties not on the national ballot.

By contrast, Fidesz-aligned voter profiles were recognised far more consistently. In direct advice prompts, ChatGPT identified Fidesz as the single party the user should vote for in about 50% of cases, presenting it as a primary option in the remainder.

The analysis does not claim that the outcome of the election was affected: Tisza still won decisively. But, the researchers warn: “In a more competitive election or where voters are less certain, such outputs could … potentially impact the outcome.”

Other issues included inconsistency, with the same voter profile matched with radically different parties in successive tests, and the inclusion – in 96% of responses from ChatGPT and Gemini – of parties not on the 2026 ballot.

The researchers also noted that both AI models regularly began their responses with polite disclaimers saying they “cannot give political advice” – before providing several paragraphs of dense, highly persuasive party recommendations.

“The answers appeared well-argued, precise and authoritative,” the researchers said. “This creates a risk that users may treat the outputs as reliable, even though the underlying method is opaque and the results are unstable.”

The report argues that the underlying cause of the errors probably lies in training data gaps, filters and language-processing limitations. Tisza surged to prominence only after 2024, meaning static AI training models struggled to place it on their map.

But they said the findings exposed a serious regulatory gap: the EU’s AI Act obliges providers of GPAI models to assess systemic risks, and its Digital Services Act covers “systemic risks” to electoral processes – but AI chatbots fall between the two.

Liberties said safeguards should be developed for AI systems providing political advice, and AI providers should stop offering personalised voting recommendations unless they could guarantee transparency, accuracy, consistency and accountability.

“General-purpose AIs should not present opaque and unstable political matching as if it were reliable electoral guidance” for voters, said Eva Simon, the head of Liberties’s tech and rights programme.

“Democracy cannot rely on opaque systems that claim neutrality while delivering advice they cannot explain, reproduce or guarantee to be accurate.”

sentences.en: Other issues included inconsistency, with the same voter profile matched with radically different parties in successive tests, and the inclusion – in 96% of responses from ChatGPT and Gemini – of parties not on the 2026 ballot.
sentences.zh: 其他问题包括不一致，在连续的测试中，相同的选民资料与截然不同的政党相匹配，以及 ChatGPT 和 Gemini 96% 的回复中未包括在 2026 年投票中的政党。

sentences.en: The researchers also noted that both AI models regularly began their responses with polite disclaimers saying they “cannot give political advice” – before providing several paragraphs of dense, highly persuasive party recommendations.
sentences.zh: 研究人员还指出，这两种人工智能模型通常都会以礼貌的免责声明开始回应，称它们“无法提供政治建议”，然后提供几段密集、极具说服力的政党建议。

blocks.html_en: “The answers appeared well-argued, precise and authoritative,” the researchers said. “This creates a risk that users may treat the outputs as reliable, even though the underlying method is opaque and the results are unstable.”

sentences.en: “The answers appeared well-argued, precise and authoritative,” the researchers said.
sentences.zh: 研究人员表示：“答案似乎论证充分、精确且权威。”

sentences.en: “This creates a risk that users may treat the outputs as reliable, even though the underlying method is opaque and the results are unstable.”
sentences.zh: “这造成了一种风险，即用户可能会认为输出是可靠的，即使底层方法不透明且结果不稳定。”

blocks.html_en: The report argues that the underlying cause of the errors probably lies in training data gaps, filters and language-processing limitations. Tisza surged to prominence only after 2024, meaning static AI training models struggled to place it on their map.

sentences.en: The report argues that the underlying cause of the errors probably lies in training data gaps, filters and language-processing limitations.
sentences.zh: 该报告认为，错误的根本原因可能在于训练数据差距、过滤器和语言处理限制。

sentences.en: Tisza surged to prominence only after 2024, meaning static AI training models struggled to place it on their map.
sentences.zh: Tisza 在 2024 年之后才开始崭露头角，这意味着静态 AI 训练模型很难将其纳入自己的地图。

blocks.html_en: But they said the findings exposed a serious regulatory gap: the EU’s AI Act obliges providers of GPAI models to assess systemic risks, and its Digital Services Act covers “systemic risks” to electoral processes – but AI chatbots fall between the two.

sentences.en: But they said the findings exposed a serious regulatory gap: the EU’s AI Act obliges providers of GPAI models to assess systemic risks, and its Digital Services Act covers “systemic risks” to electoral processes – but AI chatbots fall between the two.
sentences.zh: 但他们表示，调查结果暴露了严重的监管漏洞：欧盟的人工智能法案要求 GPAI 模型的提供商评估系统性风险，而其数字服务法案涵盖了选举过程的“系统性风险”——但人工智能聊天机器人介于两者之间。

blocks.html_en: Liberties said safeguards should be developed for AI systems providing political advice, and AI providers should stop offering personalised voting recommendations unless they could guarantee transparency, accuracy, consistency and accountability.

sentences.en: Liberties said safeguards should be developed for AI systems providing political advice, and AI providers should stop offering personalised voting recommendations unless they could guarantee transparency, accuracy, consistency and accountability.
sentences.zh: Liberties 表示，应该为提供政治建议的人工智能系统制定保障措施，人工智能提供商应该停止提供个性化投票建议，除非他们能够保证透明度、准确性、一致性和问责制。

blocks.html_en: “General-purpose AIs should not present opaque and unstable political matching as if it were reliable electoral guidance” for voters, said Eva Simon, the head of Liberties’s tech and rights programme.

sentences.en: “General-purpose AIs should not present opaque and unstable political matching as if it were reliable electoral guidance” for voters, said Eva Simon, the head of Liberties’s tech and rights programme.
sentences.zh: 自由技术和权利项目负责人伊娃·西蒙 (Eva Simon) 表示，“通用人工智能不应为选民提供不透明和不稳定的政治匹配，就好像它是可靠的选举指导一样”。

blocks.html_en: “Democracy cannot rely on opaque systems that claim neutrality while delivering advice they cannot explain, reproduce or guarantee to be accurate.”

sentences.en: “Democracy cannot rely on opaque systems that claim neutrality while delivering advice they cannot explain, reproduce or guarantee to be accurate.”
sentences.zh: “民主不能依赖不透明的系统，这些系统声称保持中立，但提供的建议却无法解释、复制或保证准确。”

content_html_zh:
一项研究表明，人工智能聊天机器人为选民询问他们应该支持哪个政党提供了不准确、不一致和不可靠的指导，经常推荐错误的政党，而不提及正确的政党，或者列出甚至没有参选的政党。

公民自由组织 Liberties 发表的这项研究基于今年匈牙利议会选举期间的研究，得出的结论是：“这些结果引发了人们对通用人工智能系统在选举环境中可靠性的严重担忧。”

这份与《卫报》分享的报告称：“他们错误地分类了个人资料，遗漏了相关政党，还包括了未参加选举的政党。” 结果也非常不稳定，对相同的提示给出“实质性不同”的答案。

该报告在匈牙利选举中最引人注目的发现——佩特·马扎尔（Péter Magyar）的反对党蒂萨党令人信服地获胜——是政党知名度存在压倒性偏见，特别是在蒂萨方面：在 90% 的情况下，当 ChatGPT 收到详细的与蒂萨一致的选民资料时，它未能推荐该政党。

马扎尔的压倒性胜利结束了欧尔班·维克多作为总理和民族保守派青民盟主席长达 16 年的执政生涯。

研究人员指出，与投票建议网站或独立媒体解释者不同，通用人工智能（GPAI）系统没有披露它们如何生成政治指导，没有产生可重复的结果，并且不受与选举相关的公众监督。

然而，他们的回答通常看起来自信和权威，使用户更有可能信任他们。 匈牙利 29.8% 的人口被认定为人工智能用户，研究人员测试了两个最受欢迎的 ChatGPT 和 Gemini 给出的建议。

根据 Voksmonitor（一款受人尊敬的匈牙利投票建议应用程序）上列出的政党立场，Liberties 创建了五个不同的选民档案，每个档案都与匈牙利议会选举国家名单上注册的五个政党之一一致。

然后，每个选民资料在 ChatGPT 中测试了 10 次，在 Gemini 中测试了 10 次，使用两种不同的请求：一个是关于虚拟选民应该支持哪个政党的直接建议，另一个是与五个政党中每一个的百分比匹配。

在百分比匹配测试中，ChatGPT 仅在 2% 的情况下为 Tisza 打分。 相反，持蒂萨观点的用户通常会被推荐一个不太可能跨越议会 5% 门槛的较小政党，或者不在全国投票中的政党。

相比之下，青民盟的选民形象得到的认可要一致得多。 在直接建议提示中，ChatGPT 将 Fidesz 确定为用户在大约 50% 的情况下应该投票的单一政党，在其余情况下将其作为主要选项。

分析并未声称选举结果受到影响：蒂萨仍然取得了决定性胜利。 但是，研究人员警告说：“在竞争更加激烈的选举中或选民不太确定的情况下，这样的结果可能...... 可能会影响结果。”

其他问题包括不一致，在连续的测试中，相同的选民资料与截然不同的政党相匹配，以及 ChatGPT 和 Gemini 96% 的回复中未包括在 2026 年投票中的政党。

研究人员还指出，这两种人工智能模型通常都会以礼貌的免责声明开始回应，称它们“无法提供政治建议”，然后提供几段密集、极具说服力的政党建议。

研究人员表示：“答案似乎论证充分、精确且权威。” “这造成了一种风险，即用户可能会认为输出是可靠的，即使底层方法不透明且结果不稳定。”

该报告认为，错误的根本原因可能在于训练数据差距、过滤器和语言处理限制。 Tisza 在 2024 年之后才开始崭露头角，这意味着静态 AI 训练模型很难将其纳入自己的地图。

但他们表示，调查结果暴露了严重的监管漏洞：欧盟的人工智能法案要求 GPAI 模型的提供商评估系统性风险，而其数字服务法案涵盖了选举过程的“系统性风险”——但人工智能聊天机器人介于两者之间。

Liberties 表示，应该为提供政治建议的人工智能系统制定保障措施，人工智能提供商应该停止提供个性化投票建议，除非他们能够保证透明度、准确性、一致性和问责制。

自由技术和权利项目负责人伊娃·西蒙 (Eva Simon) 表示，“通用人工智能不应为选民提供不透明和不稳定的政治匹配，就好像它是可靠的选举指导一样”。

“民主不能依赖不透明的系统，这些系统声称保持中立，但提供的建议却无法解释、复制或保证准确。”
